title: Pods stuck Pending — over-requested CPU
scenario: The `compute` Deployment needs 3 replicas but the pods are stuck in `Pending`. The cluster has two nodes, each with 2 CPU allocatable (4 CPU total). Each pod currently requests 1500m CPU — 3 replicas would need 4500m, which exceeds what's available. Reduce the CPU request so all three pods can schedule.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: compute
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: compute
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: compute
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: worker
        image: alpine:3.18
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "while true; do echo working; sleep 10; done"]
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "1500m"
            memory: "128Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "2000m"
            memory: "256Mi"

objectives.description: Container CPU request is `800m` or less

hints.text: Run `kubectl describe pod <pending-pod>` and look at the Events section. You will see a message like `0/2 nodes are available: 2 Insufficient cpu`.
Total cluster CPU is 4000m (2 nodes × 2000m). Divide by 3 replicas: each pod can request at most ~1333m. Aim lower — try 600m to leave headroom for system pods.
Change `resources.requests.cpu` from `1500m` to `600m` and also reduce the limit to `1000m`. Then apply. The scheduler will place the pods across both nodes.

solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: compute
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: compute
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: compute
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: worker
        image: alpine:3.18
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "while true; do echo working; sleep 10; done"]
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "600m"
            memory: "128Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "1000m"
            memory: "256Mi"
